static.description: A polyglot microservices platform for food delivery, built with 5 independent services (API Gateway, Order, Inventory, Driver, Notification) across Kotlin, Java, Python, Go, and TypeScript stacks, communicating via RabbitMQ events and fronted by a JWT-authenticated API Gateway.
domain_concepts: OrderStatus: Enum representing the lifecycle states of an order: PENDING → ACCEPTED → PREPARING → READY_FOR_PICKUP → IN_TRANSIT → DELIVERED, with CANCELLED and REFUNDED as terminal/side states.
OrderItem: An embeddable JPA entity representing a line item within an order, containing menu item ID, name, quantity, and unit price.
MenuItem: A SQLAlchemy model representing a restaurant menu item with stock_quantity (-1 means unlimited) and availability tracking.
Driver: A MongoDB document representing a delivery driver with name, phone, vehicle type, availability status, and GPS location.
GeoPoint: A struct/document embedded in Driver storing latitude and longitude coordinates for real-time location tracking.
JwtAuthFilter: A Spring Cloud Gateway global filter that validates JWT tokens and forwards the verified customer ID as an X-Customer-Id header to downstream services.
acquire_lock: A Redis-based distributed lock function in the Inventory Service that prevents concurrent stock overselling by acquiring an exclusive lock on a menu item.
order.events: The RabbitMQ exchange name used for publishing order lifecycle events that the Notification Service consumes.
stripePaymentIntentId: The Stripe PaymentIntent ID (pi_xxxx) stored on an Order, used for manual capture and refund operations.
Restaurant: A SQLAlchemy model representing a restaurant with name, address, cuisine type, rating, and associated menu items.
isValidTransition: A static method on OrderStatus that enforces the allowed state machine transitions for order lifecycle.

dynamic.architecture_summary: The platform follows a microservices architecture with an API Gateway (Kotlin/Spring Cloud Gateway) handling JWT authentication and routing to three backend services: Order Service (Java/Spring Boot with PostgreSQL and Stripe payments), Inventory Service (Python/FastAPI with PostgreSQL and Redis distributed locks), and Driver Service (Go with MongoDB). A Notification Service (TypeScript/Node.js) consumes RabbitMQ events from the order lifecycle to send SMS via Twilio and email via SendGrid. All services communicate asynchronously through RabbitMQ for order lifecycle events.

key_components.name: GatewayApplication
key_components.responsibility: Entry point for the Spring Cloud Gateway that routes requests to downstream services.

key_components.name: JwtAuthFilter
key_components.responsibility: Global gateway filter that validates JWT tokens and injects X-Customer-Id header for downstream services.

key_components.name: OrderServiceApplication
key_components.responsibility: Spring Boot entry point for the Order Service.

key_components.name: OrderController
key_components.responsibility: REST controller handling order CRUD, status transitions, and cancellation endpoints.

key_components.name: OrderService
key_components.responsibility: Core business logic for order creation with Stripe payment, status transitions with validation, cancellation/refund, and RabbitMQ event publishing.

key_components.name: Order
key_components.responsibility: JPA entity representing a delivery order with embedded line items, Stripe payment intent, and lifecycle status.

key_components.name: OrderStatus
key_components.responsibility: Enum defining the order lifecycle states and valid state transitions.

key_components.name: StripePaymentGateway
key_components.responsibility: Live Stripe SDK implementation for creating payment intents and refunds.

key_components.name: PaymentGateway
key_components.responsibility: Interface abstracting payment operations for testability without live Stripe connections.

key_components.name: inventory-service/main.py
key_components.responsibility: FastAPI application handling restaurant/menu queries and stock reservation with Redis distributed locks.

key_components.name: models.py
key_components.responsibility: SQLAlchemy ORM models for Restaurant and MenuItem with stock management logic.

key_components.name: driver-service main.go
key_components.responsibility: Go HTTP server for driver profile CRUD and real-time location tracking, backed by MongoDB.

key_components.name: notification-service index.ts
key_components.responsibility: Node.js/TypeScript service consuming RabbitMQ order events and dispatching SMS (Twilio) and email (SendGrid) notifications with idempotency.

agent_safe_operations: Add new REST endpoints to existing service controllers
Add new unit tests or integration tests in existing test directories
Modify business logic in service layer classes (OrderService, inventory main.py, driver main.go)
Add new domain models or DTOs
Update application configuration in application.yml or application.properties
Add new utility functions or helper modules
Refactor existing code within a single service boundary
agent_forbidden_operations: Modify .env or .env.example with real secrets or credentials
Change database migration schemas or JPA entity table structures without review
Change Stripe API key configuration or payment logic without explicit approval
Change JWT secret configuration or authentication bypass logic
Modify the openapi.yaml specification without corresponding code changes
Remove or weaken idempotency guards in the notification service
Change RabbitMQ exchange/queue naming conventions that would break cross-service communication
potential_pitfalls: Each service uses a different language and build tool — changes must use the correct toolchain (Maven for order-service, Gradle for api-gateway, pip for inventory-service, go modules for driver-service, npm for notification-service)
The Order model uses Lombok annotations (@Getter, @Setter, @Builder) — IDEs/agents must recognize these generate code at compile time
OrderStatus.isValidTransition enforces a strict state machine — any new status requires updating this switch expression
The inventory service uses async SQLAlchemy (asyncpg) — all database operations must use async/await patterns
Redis distributed locks in inventory-service have a TTL of 10 seconds — long-running stock operations could lose the lock
The driver-service tests use a mockStore interface — adding new DriverStore methods requires updating the mock in main_test.go
The notification-service uses in-memory idempotency (Set) — this is lost on restart and the code comments note Redis should be used in production
The api-gateway JwtAuthFilter has PUBLIC_PATHS that bypass auth — adding new public endpoints requires updating this list
Cross-service communication relies on RabbitMQ exchange 'order.events' with specific routing key patterns — changing these breaks the notification service
The inventory-service uses pydantic-settings for config — environment variable names must match the Settings field names exactly
Go driver-service uses primitive.ObjectID from MongoDB — IDs must be valid MongoDB ObjectIDs, not UUIDs
